Scenario:
` XML:
```xml
CostaRicto Group's Spearphishing Campaign Against Healthcare Organizations
Healthcare and Public Health Simple The CostaRicto group, known for their expert-level skills, is targeting healthcare organizations using spearphishing techniques to gain initial access and ultimately exfiltrate sensitive data.
name=CostaRicto Financial Gain Expert
Healthcare and Public Health Users Email
AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Users, Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases
AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Phishing, Web
Spearphishing Attachment Users Email CVE-2023-42916 [CWE-22]: Vulnerability allowing remote code execution via malicious attachment CAPEC ID: 511 - Infiltration of Software Development Environment The CostaRicto group sends targeted emails to healthcare employees containing malicious attachments exploiting CVE-2023-42916. Once opened, the attachment executes malicious code, providing the attackers with initial access to the victim's device.
PowerShell Users Web CVE-2015-1770 [CWE-94]: Vulnerability allowing unauthorized modification of Windows registry CAPEC ID: 579 - Replace Winlogon Helper DLL After gaining initial access, the attackers use PowerShell to establish command and control (C2) communication with the compromised system. They leverage CVE-2015-1770 to modify the Windows registry, ensuring their presence persists after system reboots.
Data Exfiltration over Command and Control Channel Web server services such as emails and databases Web N/A CAPEC ID: 24 - Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow With persistence established, the CostaRicto group exfiltrates sensitive patient data, intellectual property, or other valuable information stored on the compromised web servers using their command and control channel.
The CostaRicto group employs a simple yet effective attack chain, starting with spearphishing to gain initial access, followed by using PowerShell for persistence and command and control, culminating in the exfiltration of sensitive data from compromised web servers.
